food_subgroup: Chinese street food / Shaanxi flatbread sandwich

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Roujiamo. The closest defensible FoodOn anchor is 'sandwich food product' (FOODON_03315595), used as a closest_parent match for this flatbread-and-meat sandwich.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP (meat sandwich/burger) is the closest defensible FoodEx2 anchor for Roujiamo. No exact FoodEx2 entry exists for this traditional Chinese flatbread sandwich.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for Roujiamo or a sufficiently close analogue.

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard serving of Roujiamo is one whole sandwich piece weighing approximately 150 g, consisting of one Baijimo flatbread stuffed with a generous portion of braised and chopped spiced pork.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Pork sandwich / meat-filled flatbread composite; adapted from SR Legacy entries for braised pork (NDB 10006) and wheat flatbread (NDB 18033)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA FoodData Central entry exists for Roujiamo. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy data for braised pork belly (NDB 10006) and wheat flatbread / Chinese-style steamed/baked bread (NDB 18033), combined in an approximate 45% bread / 55% pork ratio consistent with standard Roujiamo preparation. Input AI-generated values were cross-checked against this composite and retained where consistent. Saturated fat estimated from USDA pork belly and bread fat profiles. Monounsaturated, polyunsaturated, trans fat, cholesterol, and water data not reliably derivable for this composite and are set to -1.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of wheat flatbread and mixed pork-bread sandwich — analogous estimate based on white wheat bread GI reference
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Roujiamo in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 60 (medium) is estimated by analogy with white wheat flatbread / pita-style bread entries in the University of Sydney GI Database (GI range 57–68 for wheat flatbreads), moderated downward by the substantial protein (12.5 g/100g) and fat (14.5 g/100g) content of the pork filling, which is well-established to attenuate postprandial glycemic response. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (60 × 39.75) / 100 ≈ 23.8, where available carbohydrate per 150 g serving = 26.5 × 1.5 = 39.75 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (480 mg/100 g; ~720 mg per standard 150 g serving) from the spiced stewing brine and seasoning
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit Roujiamo consumption due to its high sodium load (~720 mg per serving, approximately 31% of the 2300 mg daily limit), and should request reduced-salt preparation or consume only half a serving to manage blood pressure.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic index (GI 60) with medium glycemic load (~23.8 per serving); carbohydrate content partially offset by high protein and fat
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes can consume Roujiamo in moderation, as the protein and fat from the pork filling slow gastric emptying and moderate the glycemic response, but should monitor total carbohydrate intake (~40 g per 150 g serving) and avoid pairing with other high-carbohydrate foods in the same meal.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated saturated fat (~4.8 g/100 g) and high sodium (480 mg/100 g) from pork belly and seasoned brine
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit Roujiamo to occasional consumption, as the saturated fat from pork belly and the high sodium content both contribute to cardiovascular risk; choosing leaner pork cuts and requesting reduced-salt preparation can partially mitigate these concerns.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Anti-inflammatory spices (ginger, cinnamon) present, but high saturated fat and refined flour may be pro-inflammatory
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Roujiamo contains ginger and cinnamon with documented anti-inflammatory properties, but the high saturated fat from pork belly and refined wheat flour may promote systemic inflammation; individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should consume in moderation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Roujiamo (肉夹馍), commonly known as the 'Chinese Hamburger', is one of China's most iconic street foods and is considered among the world's oldest sandwich-style dishes, with origins in Shaanxi Province dating back over two millennia. The dish consists of two main components: a hand-made, pan-baked wheat flatbread called Baijimo, and a filling of tender, highly aromatic braised pork. The Baijimo is notable for its distinctive chrysanthemum-patterned crust, crispy exterior, and dense, chewy interior that readily absorbs the savory juices of the meat. The pork — traditionally pork belly — is slow-stewed for several hours in a rich, multi-spice brine (Laozhi) containing ginger, cloves, star anise, cinnamon, soy sauce, and other aromatics until fall-apart tender. Just before serving, the meat is finely chopped, often with a splash of the stewing liquid, and sometimes mixed with green peppers or fresh cilantro for brightness. Nutritionally, a 150 g serving provides approximately 428 kcal, 18.8 g protein, 21.8 g fat, and 39.8 g carbohydrate. Sodium content is high at approximately 720 mg per serving, making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index is estimated at 60 (medium), moderated by the substantial protein and fat content. Roujiamo is a popular breakfast, lunch, and snack food throughout China, particularly in Xi'an and the broader Shaanxi region, and has gained widespread popularity across China and internationally as a satisfying, portable street food.
common_user_queries: What spices are in Roujiamo pork?
What is the GI of Roujiamo?

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Roujiamo exists in any approved primary source (USDA FoodData Central, HPB Singapore, ICMR-NIN, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, FAO/INFOODS SEA). Nutrient values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy composite data for braised pork belly and wheat flatbread in standard proportions, cross-checked against the AI-generated input values which were broadly consistent. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database wheat flatbread entries. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. Nutrient values estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy composite (braised pork belly NDB 10006 + wheat flatbread NDB 18033, ~55:45 pork:bread ratio). No exact approved-source entry for Roujiamo exists. Input AI-generated values were retained where consistent with the USDA composite. 2. Saturated fat (4.8 g/100g) estimated from USDA pork belly and wheat bread fat profiles. 3. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractionation and moisture data for this traditional braised-pork flatbread composite are not available in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy or any other approved source as of 2026-04-17. 4. GI of 60 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database wheat flatbread entries (GI 57–68), moderated by the high protein and fat content of the pork filling. 5. Glycemic load per 150 g serving calculated as (60 × 39.75) / 100 ≈ 23.8 g. 6. The input source cited ('Shaanxi Normal University') is not on the approved source list and has not been used as primary evidence. 7. Duplicate portion entry in input (two identical 150 g entries) — deduplicated to a single 150 g serving. 8. This dish is not a Singaporean dish; multilingual requirements for Malay, Tamil, and Hindi do not apply. Chinese-script names are provided as the dish is Chinese in origin.
